sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 15.12 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.15)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a group acting faithfully and level-transitively by automorphisms on a rooted tree$T$. For a vertex$v$of$T$, the rigid vertex stabilizer\/ at$v$consists of those elements of$G$whose support in$T$lies entirely in the subtree${ T}_v$rooted at$v$. For a non-negative integer$n$, the$n$-th rigid level stabilizer\/ is the subgroup of$G$generated by all rigid vertex stabilizers corresponding to ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
